channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: American surfer Bethany Hamilton seeks support, treatment advice for hospitalized family member
item.description: Bethany Hamilton survived a shark attack and went on to became a pro surfer, she is now doing everything she can to help her nephew overcome his own set of challenges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CMU women's soccer is on fire and feeling good
item.description: Following a huge 2-1 road win over MSU Denver, Colorado Mesa women's soccer has won five games in a row.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Tom Brady's purchase of a minority stake in the Las Vegas Raiders is approved by NFL team owners
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Tom Brady has another new role in the NFL: owner. The seven-time Super Bowl champion purchased a minority stake in the Las Vegas Raiders, a deal unanimously approved by NFL team owners on Tuesday at the league's annual fall meeting.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL owners approve Jacksonville's $1.4 billion 'stadium of the future' set to open in 2028
item.description: NFL owners unanimously approved Jacksonville's $1.4 billion “stadium of the future” Tuesday, a decision that should keep the Jaguars in one of the league's smallest markets for at least another 30 years.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to watch the U.S. men’s soccer team vs. Mexico Tuesday night
item.description: The United States men's soccer team takes on Mexico in an international friendly at 7:30 p.m. PDT on Tuesday, Oct. 15.
item.dc:creator: Pueng Vongs, The Mercury News

item.title: San Jose State volleyball at the center of another decision on forfeiting
item.description: The University of Nevada has made it clear that its Oct_ 26 volleyball match against San Jose State will go on as scheduled after the team’s players released an independent statement saying they would “refuse to participate in any match that advances injustice against female athletes.”.
item.dc:creator: Mark Anderson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Golden State Valkyries name team's first head coach
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Twenty years after playing professional women’s basketball in San Jose, Natalie Nakase is returning to the Bay Area after being named Thursday as the Golden State Valkyries' first head coach.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, KRON4

item.title: PGA Golfers impressed with Black Desert Resort
item.description: IVINS, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - The PGA Tour returns to Utah for the first time since 1963 when the Black Desert Championship tees off Thursday in Ivins, Utah. Most of the 132 golfers in the tournament have never played in Utah before, and they have been impressed with the course at Black Desert Resort.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ahead of team's game in London, Bears president says he remains steadfast on proceeding with plans for new lakefront stadium
item.description: WARE, England (AP) — The Bears remain focused on the city’s lakefront as the location for a nearly $5 billion stadium development project, team president and CEO Kevin Warren reiterated Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Tropicana demolition in Las Vegas makes way for A's stadium
item.description: (KRON) -- Tropicana Las Vegas was demolished early Wednesday morning, making way for a proposed baseball stadium for the Athletics. The Tropicana implosion happened at around 2:30 a.m. on the Las Vegas strip, with the A’s logo lighting up the sky as both hotel towers came down.
item.dc:creator: Michael Thomas, KRON4

item.title: Tatis homers again as the Padres beat the Dodgers 6-5 for a 2-1 NLDS lead
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Fernando Tatis Jr.'s towering two-run home run highlighted a six-run second inning, and the San Diego Padres held on to beat Shohei Ohtani and the rival Los Angeles Dodgers 6-5 on Tuesday night to take a 2-1 lead in a tense NL Division Series.
item.dc:creator: BERNIE WILSON, Associated Press, KTLA 5
